data.title: GREEN: Fix bridge-inbound to write local LINK events for Jira Relates links
data.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Description
bridge-inbound.py currently calls acli_client.set_relationship() for inbound Jira issue links (lines 848-880), which pushes links BACK to Jira instead of writing local LINK events. For "Relates" link types, it should write a local LINK event file with relation="relates_to" so the local ticket system tracks the relationship.

## Implementation Details
1. In the issuelinks processing block (lines 848-880), for links with type.name == "Relates":
   - Instead of calling set_relationship(), write a LINK event JSON file in the ticket directory
   - Event format: {event_type: "LINK", ticket_id: <local_id>, timestamp: <now>, data: {source_id: <local_id>, target_id: <target_local_id>, relation: "relates_to"}, env_id: <bridge_env_id>}
   - Resolve target_local_id from the target Jira key (format: "jira-<key-lowercase>")
   - Keep the existing set_relationship behavior for non-Relates link types (Blocks, etc.)
2. Remove the @pytest.mark.xfail marker from test_inbound_round_trip_jira_relates_link_creates_local_relates_to in test_bridge_inbound_integration.py
3. Verify the round-trip test passes

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/scripts/bridge-inbound.py (Edit: modify issuelinks processing)
- tests/scripts/test_bridge_inbound_integration.py (Edit: remove xfail marker)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Inbound Relates links write local LINK events instead of calling set_relationship
  Verify: grep -q "LINK" plugins/dso/scripts/bridge-inbound.py
- [ ] xfail marker removed from inbound round-trip test
  Verify: ! grep -q "xfail" tests/scripts/test_bridge_inbound_integration.py
- [ ] Inbound round-trip test passes
  Verify: python3 -m pytest tests/scripts/test_bridge_inbound_integration.py -k "round_trip" --tb=short -q
